"   KNA1 (customer general) + KNB1 (customer company code)

" Verify table fields in SE11:
SELECT SINGLE * FROM dd03l
  WHERE tabname = 'ACDOCA' AND fieldname = 'DMBTR'
  INTO @DATA(ls_field).
IF sy-subrc = 0. WRITE: / 'Field confirmed'. ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you translate FS performance requirements into TS for an ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS performance requirements specific TS decisions mein translate karte hain. 'Under 30 seconds' yeh banta hai: warning ke saath 10,000 ki row limit, GROUP BY ke through database level pe pushed aggregation, secondary table joins ke liye FOR ALL ENTRIES SELECT inside loop eliminate karte hue, aur kisi bhi remaining large fetches ke liye PACKAGE SIZE. TS production se pehle mandatory step ke roop mein performance testing bhi specify karta hai — DEV ya QA mein production-equivalent data volume ke saath test karo, SLA ke against actual execution time measure karo aur result document karo. Agar SLA meet nahi ho sakti, TS alternatives propose karta hai — detail-level ke bajaaye summary-level reporting, ya S/4HANA mein HANA-optimized execution ke liye CDS-based analytical query.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FS performance requirements translate to specific TS decisions. 'Under 30 seconds' becomes: row limit of 10,000 with warning, aggregation pushed to database level via GROUP BY, FOR ALL ENTRIES for secondary table joins eliminating SELECT inside loop, and PACKAGE SIZE for any remaining large fetches. The TS also specifies performance testing as a mandatory step before production — test with production-equivalent data volume in DEV or QA, measure actual execution time against the SLA, and document the result. If the SLA cannot be met, the TS proposes alternatives — summary-level reporting instead of detail-level, or CDS-based analytical query for HANA-optimized execution in S/4HANA.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS requirement: 'Vendor aging must run in under 2 minutes.' TS design: aggregate aging buckets directly in SELECT with SUM and CASE — no ABAP loop summation. FOR ALL ENTRIES for vendor name. PACKAGE SIZE 10000 for BSIK fetch. Performance test in QA with 500,000 open items — result: 47 seconds. Adjustment: added parallel cursor for BSIK sort — reduced to 28 seconds. Documented: 'Performance SLA 2 minutes — achieved 28 seconds on QA volume equivalent to PRD.'
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Writing performance requirement in TS as 'use efficient coding practices' — this is meaningless. TS must be specific: row limit value, specific join strategy, specific aggregation approach, specific test data volume, and specific measured target time. Vague TS leads to vague code.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS performance specification → code implementation
" TS says: 'HANA aggregation, no ABAP loop sum, FAE for joins'

" Database aggregation — TS specified approach
SELECT lifnr, bukrs,
       SUM( CASE WHEN ( @sy-datum - bldat ) <= 30 THEN dmbtr ELSE 0 END )
         AS bucket0_30,
       SUM( CASE WHEN ( @sy-datum - bldat ) BETWEEN 31 AND 60
         THEN dmbtr ELSE 0 END ) AS bucket31_60,
       SUM( CASE WHEN ( @sy-datum - bldat ) > 60 THEN dmbtr ELSE 0 END )
         AS bucket61plus
  FROM bsik
  WHERE bukrs IN @s_bukrs AND lifnr IN @s_lifnr
  GROUP BY lifnr, bukrs
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_aging)
  UP TO 10000 ROWS.  " TS specified row limit

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle calculated fields in the TS for an ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SAP tables mein directly available nahi hone wale calculated fields TS mein explicitly designed hone chahiye. Aging buckets ke liye, TS define karta hai: bucket fields ke saath custom TYPE structure currency ke saath packed decimal amounts ke roop mein, calculation formula — key date se subtracted document date days outstanding deta hai — aur CASE ya IF use karke bucket assignment logic. TS yeh bhi specify karta hai ki calculation database expressions use karke SELECT mein hota hai ya ABAP post-fetch mein. S/4HANA ke liye, SELECT mein CASE expressions calculation ko HANA tak push karte hain — preferred approach. TS calculated fields ke liye field catalog setup bhi define karta hai: fieldname, column header, currency reference ke liye CFIELDNAME, totals enabled ke liye DO_SUM.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Calculated fields not directly available in SAP tables must be explicitly designed in the TS. For aging buckets, the TS defines: custom TYPE structure with bucket fields as packed decimal amounts with currency, the calculation formula — document date subtracted from key date gives days outstanding — and bucket assignment logic using CASE or IF. The TS also specifies whether calculation happens in the SELECT using database expressions or in ABAP post-fetch. For S/4HANA, CASE expressions in SELECT push calculation to HANA — preferred approach. The TS also defines the field catalog setup for calculated fields: fieldname, column header, CFIELDNAME for currency reference, DO_SUM for totals enabled.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS: 'Days overdue column for each invoice.' TS decision: DATEDIFF function in SELECT — DATEDIFF( DAY, bldat, @sy-datum ) AS days_overdue — HANA native function. Output type: integer. Field catalog: DAYS_OVERDUE column, no currency reference needed. Visual: right-align integer, highlight red if > 30. This design decision in TS saved 2 days of ABAP loop coding compared to post-fetch calculation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Leaving calculated field approach undefined in TS with only 'calculate aging bucket as needed.' Developer then makes ad-hoc choices — maybe SELECT inside LOOP — causing performance issues. TS must specify calculation approach explicitly including whether it is database-side or ABAP-side.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS calculated fields → implementation
" Custom TYPE with bucket fields
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_aging_output,
         lifnr      TYPE lfa1-lifnr,
         name1      TYPE lfa1-name1,
         bucket0_30  TYPE bsik-dmbtr,  " 0-30 days
         bucket31_60 TYPE bsik-dmbtr,  " 31-60 days
         bucket61_90 TYPE bsik-dmbtr,  " 61-90 days
         bucket91    TYPE bsik-dmbtr,  " 90+ days
         waers       TYPE bsik-waers,  " Currency ref
       END OF ty_aging_output.

" ABAP calculation (alternative to HANA CASE)
DATA(lv_days) = sy-datum - ls_bsik-bldat.
CASE abap_true.
  WHEN lv_days <= 30.             ls_out-bucket0_30  += ls_bsik-dmbtr.
  WHEN lv_days BETWEEN 31 AND 60. ls_out-bucket31_60 += ls_bsik-dmbtr.
  WHEN lv_days BETWEEN 61 AND 90. ls_out-bucket61_90 += ls_bsik-dmbtr.
  WHEN OTHERS.                    ls_out-bucket91    += ls_bsik-dmbtr.
ENDCASE.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you specify authorization requirements in a TS for an ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ALV report ke liye TS mein authorization requirements specific hone chahiye: konsa authorization object, konse fields, konsa activity code, aur hard-fail karna hai ya silently filter. Example TS: 'F_BKPF_BUK OBJECT ke saath AUTHORITY-CHECK with BUKRS = selection screen se company code aur ACTVT = 03 data fetch se pehle. Agar check fail karta hai, E message raise karo aur execution stop karo.' Un reports ke liye jahan users kuch lekin saare company codes ke liye authorized ho sakte hain — multi-org reports — TS TVKO ke through loop ke saath saare authorized company codes collect karne aur har ek check karne ko specify karta hai, phir data ko filter karke sirf authorized entries include karne ke liye. Basis team ko report ke liye authorization roles create ya update karne ke liye yeh TS information chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Authorization requirements in TS for ALV reports must be specific: which authorization object, which fields, which activity code, and whether to hard-fail or silently filter. Example TS: 'AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT F_BKPF_BUK with BUKRS = company code from selection screen and ACTVT = 03 before data fetch. If check fails, raise E message and stop execution.' For reports where users may be authorized for some but not all company codes — multi-org reports — the TS specifies collecting all authorized company codes via loop through TVKO and checking each, then filtering the data to only include authorized entries. Basis team needs this TS information to create or update authorization roles for the report.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS authorization design mistake: only checked single company code authorization but user could enter multiple company codes in SELECT-OPTIONS. Fixed TS: loop through all entered company codes, AUTHORITY-CHECK each one, build authorized list, restrict SELECT to authorized list only. If any selected company code is unauthorized — message W and continue with authorized ones only. This partial authorization handling is more user-friendly than hard-stop on first unauthorized code.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Writing authorization specification in TS as just 'add required auth checks.' This is not actionable. TS must name the exact authorization object, field IDs, and activity. Without this specification, developer either skips authorization or implements the wrong object — both create security risks.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS authorization specification → code
" TS says: F_BKPF_BUK, BUKRS, ACTVT 03
" Partial auth: collect authorized company codes

DATA lt_auth_bukrs TYPE TABLE OF bukrs.
SELECT bukrs FROM t001 WHERE bukrs IN @s_bukrs
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_all_bukrs).

LOOP AT lt_all_bukrs INTO DATA(ls_bukrs).
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD ls_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    APPEND ls_bukrs TO lt_auth_bukrs.  " Only authorized ones
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

IF lt_auth_bukrs IS INITIAL.
  MESSAGE 'Not authorized for any of the selected company codes'
    TYPE 'E'. RETURN.
ENDIF.
" Use lt_auth_bukrs in SELECT — not original s_bukrs range
SELECT lifnr, bukrs, dmbtr FROM bsik
  WHERE bukrs IN @lt_auth_bukrs
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_items).

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you clarify ambiguous FS requirements before writing TS for a report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS likhne se pehle, main FS mein saari ambiguities ko functional consultant ke saath ek structured review session ke through identify aur resolve karta hoon. Report FS ke liye common ambiguity areas: 'active records' — definition chahiye — yeh ek status field, ek date validity ya ek deletion flag hai; 'current period data' — calendar month ya last N days; 'total amount' — net ya gross, konsi currency, kis date pe exchange rate; 'authorized users' — konsa role ya authorization object access control karta hai. Main har clarified point ko TS mein ek assumptions section ke roop mein ek numbered question-answer pair ke roop mein document karta hoon. Yeh developer aur functional team ke beech formal agreement ban jaata hai, UAT surprises prevent karte hue. Koi bhi unclear point jo immediately resolve nahi ho sakta development start hone se pehle ek decision deadline paata hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Before writing TS, I identify and resolve all ambiguities in the FS through a structured review session with the functional consultant. Common ambiguity areas for report FS: 'active records' — needs definition — is it a status field, a date validity, or a deletion flag; 'current period data' — calendar month or last N days; 'total amount' — net or gross, which currency, exchange rate at what date; 'authorized users' — which role or authorization object controls access. I document each clarified point as a numbered question-answer pair in the TS as an assumptions section. This becomes the formal agreement between developer and functional team, preventing UAT surprises. Any unclear point that cannot be immediately resolved gets a decision deadline before development starts.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS review session revealed: 'Show vendor balance' had 3 interpretations — open items only, cleared items only, or both. Developer assumed open items, functional wanted both. Cost: 2 days of rework. After lesson: all 'balance' related requirements now have an explicit clarification in TS with functional sign-off: 'Include BSIK (open) + BSAK (cleared) items. Date range applies to posting date BUDAT not document date BLDAT.' Both signed, no surprises at UAT.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Writing TS with assumptions not confirmed by functional team — tagging them as 'TBD' and starting development anyway. TBD assumptions almost always lead to rework. If an assumption is critical to design, it must be resolved before development begins, not during UAT.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Assumptions section — document all clarified points:
" Assumption 1: 'Active vendors' = LFB1-SPERR = space
"   (not deleted/blocked). Confirmed by: FI Functional Lead.
" Assumption 2: 'Current period' = sy-datum first day of month
"   to sy-datum. Confirmed by: FI Functional Lead.
" Assumption 3: 'Total amount' = DMBTR (local currency only).
"   Multi-currency to be addressed in Phase 2. Confirmed.
" Assumption 4: Authorization = F_BKPF_BUK ACTVT 03.
"   Basis to add to existing FI_DISPLAY role. Confirmed.

" Code based on confirmed assumptions:
SELECT lifnr FROM lfb1
  WHERE bukrs IN @s_bukrs
    AND sperr = space        " Assumption 1: active = not blocked
    AND lifnr IN @s_lifnr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_active_vendors).

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you specify the output structure in TS for a complex ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS mein output structure sabse precise section hai — yeh har column define karta hai jo user dekhega. Main har field ke technical name, data type, length aur description ke saath ek TYPES definition document karta hoon. Currency amounts ke liye, amount field aur currency key field dono included hote hain even agar currency key column hidden hoga. Calculated fields ke liye, main calculation formula aur resulting type specify karta hoon. TS field catalog settings bhi document karta hai: column header text, output width, do_sum flag, clickable fields ke liye hotspot flag, aur hidden reference columns ke liye no_out. Yeh level of detail ek peer developer ko sirf TS se exact same report build karne ki allow karta hai bina FS reference kiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Output structure in TS is the most precise section — it defines every column the user will see. I document a TYPES definition with each field's technical name, data type, length, and description. For currency amounts, both the amount field and the currency key field are included even if the currency key column will be hidden. For calculated fields, I specify the calculation formula and resulting type. TS also documents field catalog settings: column header text, output width, do_sum flag, hotspot flag for clickable fields, and no_out for hidden reference columns. This level of detail allows a peer developer to build the exact same report from the TS alone without needing to reference the FS.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS review finding: output structure had amount fields but no currency key fields — field catalog could not set CFIELDNAME properly. Added WAERK and WAERS to the TYPE definition with NO_OUT = true flag noted in TS. Also added BUCKET_CURRENCY TYPE WAERS for aging buckets with mixed currencies. After TS update, developer built correct field catalog first time without any UAT finding on currency formatting.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Defining output structure in TS as just 'same as VBAK structure' or referencing a database table directly. This leads to SELECT * tendencies and includes unnecessary columns. Always define a custom TYPE with only the fields the ALV needs — this is the blueprint for both the internal table and the field catalog.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS output structure definition → TYPE specification
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_vendor_aging,
         lifnr       TYPE lfa1-lifnr,       " Vendor number
         name1       TYPE lfa1-name1,       " Vendor name
         bukrs       TYPE lfa1-bukrs,       " Company code
         waers       TYPE waers,            " Currency (hidden ref)
         bucket0_30  TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " 0-30 days amount
         bucket31_60 TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " 31-60 days
         bucket61_90 TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " 61-90 days
         bucket91    TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " 90+ days
         total_open  TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " Grand total
       END OF ty_vendor_aging.

" TS field catalog notes (for developer reference):
" BUCKET0_30: CFIELDNAME=WAERS, DO_SUM=true, coltext='0-30 Days'
" BUCKET31_60: CFIELDNAME=WAERS, DO_SUM=true, coltext='31-60 Days'
" WAERS: NO_OUT=true (hidden currency reference column)
" TOTAL_OPEN: DO_SUM=true, coltext='Total Open', emphasis=true

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you write test cases in a TS for an ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ALV reports ke liye TS test cases scenario-specific hone chahiye, generic nahi. Main at least aath categories document karta hoon: expected row count aur sample output ke saath happy path; specific selection criteria ke saath empty result jo isse trigger karti hai; row limit ke close data count plus warning message expectation ke saath large volume; calculated fields ke liye boundary value — aging ke liye, exactly 30 days aur exactly 31 days different buckets mein; restricted user sirf authorized data dikhate hue; spool verification ke saath background job execution; numeric amount verification ke saath Excel export; aur layout variant save aur restore. Har test case mein input parameters, expected output aur specific verification steps shamil hote hain. Yeh TS test section UAT checklist ban jaata hai aur directly reused ho sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: TS test cases for ALV reports should be scenario-specific, not generic. I document at least eight categories: happy path with expected row count and sample output; empty result with the specific selection criteria that triggers it; large volume with data count close to the row limit plus the warning message expectation; boundary value for calculated fields — for aging, exactly 30 days and exactly 31 days in different buckets; restricted user showing only authorized data; background job execution with spool verification; Excel export with numeric amount verification; and layout variant save and restore. Each test case includes the input parameters, expected output, and the specific verification steps. This TS test section becomes the UAT checklist and can be directly reused.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS with specific test cases reduced UAT defects by 60% in one project. Tester had a checklist instead of guessing scenarios. Aging boundary test caught a bug — exactly 30-day items were being placed in 31-60 bucket due to <= vs < operator mismatch in calculation. TS boundary test caught it before UAT — developer fixed before even showing to business team.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Writing generic test cases like 'ensure all fields display correctly' or 'verify performance is acceptable.' These are not testable. TS test cases must have specific inputs and specific expected outputs so anyone — developer or tester — can execute and verify without ambiguity.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Test Cases — ALV Vendor Aging Report

" TC001: Happy Path
" Input: BUKRS=1000, LIFNR=* (all vendors), KEYDT=today
" Expected: rows returned, bucket columns populated,
"           grand totals match sum of individual rows
" Verify: row counts match SE16 BSIK count for same criteria

" TC002: Empty Result
" Input: BUKRS=9999 (test company code with no data)
" Expected: Status bar 'No records found for company code 9999'
"           No ALV grid displayed

" TC003: Row Limit
" Input: BUKRS=1000, LIFNR=* (large vendor base), wide date
" Expected: Warning 'Data limited to 5000 rows'
"           Exactly 5000 rows in ALV

" TC004: Boundary Value — Aging Bucket
" Input: Create test BSIK record with BLDAT = today - 30
" Expected: Record in bucket 0-30 (not 31-60)
" Input: BLDAT = today - 31
" Expected: Record in bucket 31-60

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle the FS to TS conversion when the requirement involves HANA-specific optimization?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: S/4HANA projects ke liye, TS ko explicitly HANA optimization opportunities address karne chahiye. Main specify karta hoon: financial line items ke liye BSEG ke bajaaye ACDOCA use karo — yeh S/4HANA simplification list guidance follow karta hai; aging buckets jaisi calculated columns ke liye SELECT mein HANA native CASE expressions use karo — computation ko database layer tak push karta hai; pure read-only analytical reports ke liye @Analytics.query: true se annotated CDS Analytical Query consider karo — yeh HANA columnar storage se most benefit lete hain; cleaner code ke liye older REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY function module ke bajaaye CL_SALV_TABLE use karo. TS koi bhi performance testing baseline bhi document karta hai — ECC pe existing report versus S/4HANA pe target — taaki go-live performance gain demonstrate ho sake.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For S/4HANA projects, the TS must explicitly address HANA optimization opportunities. I specify: use ACDOCA instead of BSEG for financial line items — this follows S/4HANA simplification list guidance; use HANA native CASE expressions in SELECT for calculated columns like aging buckets — pushes computation to database layer; consider CDS Analytical Query annotated with @Analytics.query: true for pure read-only analytical reports — these benefit most from HANA columnar storage; use CL_SALV_TABLE instead of the older REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY function module for cleaner code. The TS also documents any performance testing baseline — existing report on ECC versus target on S/4HANA — so go-live performance gain can be demonstrated.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: S/4HANA migration TS decision: aging report was using BSEG on ECC — 3 minutes for 500k items. TS specified ACDOCA migration with HANA aggregation. After migration: same report 8 seconds. TS documented expected performance improvement as 'target under 30 seconds' based on HANA benchmark data. Client was very impressed with the demonstrated improvement — it justified the migration cost.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Writing an S/4HANA TS that still specifies old ECC table BSEG for financial data. Developer implements as specified, performance is no better than ECC, migration value is not realized. TS must explicitly name S/4HANA replacement tables and HANA-optimized approaches — do not assume developers know the S/4HANA changes without being told in the TS.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS HANA optimization specification → code
" TS says: Use ACDOCA, push aggregation to HANA

" S/4HANA optimized: ACDOCA + HANA aggregation + CASE expression
SELECT rbukrs AS bukrs,
       racct  AS hkont,
       SUM( CASE WHEN ( @sy-datum - budat ) <= 30
              THEN dmbtr ELSE 0 END ) AS bucket0_30,
       SUM( CASE WHEN ( @sy-datum - budat ) BETWEEN 31 AND 60
              THEN dmbtr ELSE 0 END ) AS bucket31_60,
       SUM( CASE WHEN ( @sy-datum - budat ) > 60
              THEN dmbtr ELSE 0 END ) AS bucket61plus,
       SUM( dmbtr ) AS total_open
  FROM acdoca                         " S/4HANA: not BSEG
  WHERE rbukrs IN @s_bukrs
    AND rldnr   = '0L'               " Leading ledger
    AND augdt   = '00000000'         " Open items only
  GROUP BY rbukrs, racct
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_aging_hana)
  UP TO 10000 ROWS.
